data.body: EVAL GAP (user asked): the plan did NOT explicitly include constructing standing per-criterion evals from the three corpuses. What existed: E1 (seeded per-criterion recall, one-shot), E4 (rebar+snap generalization, one-shot), 6f2d WS-PROMPTS 'evaluable' + calibrate-against-human-graded-sample, F10 (vague labeled gold set), REVIEW_RESULT offline FP/FN — all scattered/implicit, none a standing cross-corpus per-criterion eval suite. ADDED WS-EVAL to epic 6f2d: a STANDING per-criterion/per-prompt eval suite in the workflow-engine eval seam (promptfoo/Inspect), labeled cases (recall/false-accept/false-fire) drawn from DSO (read-only) + snap-oakhart-manual + rebar internal tickets, eval-gating every prompt change; operationalizes F10, turns one-shot E1/E4 into a regression suite living in the prompt library (not docs/experiments, which only seed it). + AC. check-ac/clarity still pass.
